description_long: This object consists of three distinct vertical stacks of irregularly shaped, dark gray stones, each pile composed of two to three stones of varying sizes. The stones have a rugged, natural appearance with uneven surfaces, sharp edges, and visible textural details typical of weathered rock. The coloration is predominantly a deep charcoal gray, with subtle variations in tone that suggest mineral inclusions or surface weathering. The arrangement of the stones is loose and organic, as if they were manually placed or naturally tumbled into position, with some stones appearing more rounded while others are angular. The surface texture is coarse and matte, lacking any visible polish or artificial treatment. This type of stone grouping is commonly found in urban parks, roadside barriers, or as decorative landscape features in public spaces.
description: A group of three vertical stacks of dark gray, rugged stones with irregular shapes and coarse, matte surfaces. The stones are loosely arranged, resembling natural or manually placed urban landscape barriers.
description_view_0: Three vertical stacks of stones are visible from the front, showing the rounded and angular shapes of each pile with clear separation between the groups.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the depth and layering of the stones, with the leftmost stack appearing more prominent and a small, isolated stone visible on the far right.
description_view_2: From the rear, the arrangement of the stones is more apparent, with the central stack showing a single stone in front and the overall grouping forming a loose arc.
description_view_3: This angle highlights the irregularity of the stone shapes and the staggered arrangement, with the smallest stone now on the far left and the stacks receding into the background.
attribute_location: urban park, roadside, or landscaped public area
attribute_placement: loosely stacked on the ground as a barrier or decorative feature
attribute_pattern: irregular mottling and natural textural variations, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns
attribute_weathering: rough and weathered, with visible surface pitting and minor erosion indicating prolonged exposure to the elements
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other landscaping materials, often partially embedded or surrounded by low vegetation
